subject: reading
prompt:
The student wants to emphasize a difference between the two lighthouse keepers. Which choice most effectively uses relevant information from the notes to accomplish this goal?
While researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes:
• Lighthouses send out crucial light signals to help ships and other watercraft navigate at night.
• Before automation, lighthouses were run by lighthouse keepers.
• Clara Bennett Hayes was the lighthouse keeper at Monhegan Island Light in Maine.
• She held this position from 1862 to 1880.
• Nora Whitaker was the lighthouse keeper at Point Fermin Light in California.
• She held this position from 1925 to 1941.
passage:
The student wants to emphasize a difference between the two lighthouse keepers. Which choice most effectively uses relevant information from the notes to accomplish this goal?
While researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes:
• Lighthouses send out crucial light signals to help ships and other watercraft navigate at night.
• Before automation, lighthouses were run by lighthouse keepers.
• Clara Bennett Hayes was the lighthouse keeper at Monhegan Island Light in Maine.
• She held this position from 1862 to 1880.
• Nora Whitaker was the lighthouse keeper at Point Fermin Light in California.
• She held this position from 1925 to 1941.

choices.text: As the lighthouse keeper at Point Fermin Light, Nora Whitaker helped watercraft navigate at night.
From 1862 to 1880, the nighttime waters of Maine were made more navigable thanks to Clara Bennett Hayes.
Clara Bennett Hayes worked as a lighthouse keeper in an earlier century than did Nora Whitaker.
Clara Bennett Hayes and Nora Whitaker both played crucial roles in ensuring safe navigation for watercraft in past centuries.

subject: reading
prompt:
The student wants to recount the sequence of events proposed by the researchers. Which choice most effectively uses relevant information from the notes to accomplish this goal?
While researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes:
• If a moon orbiting a planet comes close enough to that planet, tidal forces can cause the moon to break apart.
• In a 2022 study, researchers proposed that Saturn was once orbited by a large moon they named Chrysalis.
• Their simulations indicated that Chrysalis would likely have come very close to Saturn around 160 million years ago.
• At that distance, Chrysalis would have been broken apart by tidal forces.
• The researchers hypothesized that the resulting debris formed Saturn's rings.
passage:
The student wants to recount the sequence of events proposed by the researchers. Which choice most effectively uses relevant information from the notes to accomplish this goal?
While researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes:
• If a moon orbiting a planet comes close enough to that planet, tidal forces can cause the moon to break apart.
• In a 2022 study, researchers proposed that Saturn was once orbited by a large moon they named Chrysalis.
• Their simulations indicated that Chrysalis would likely have come very close to Saturn around 160 million years ago.
• At that distance, Chrysalis would have been broken apart by tidal forces.
• The researchers hypothesized that the resulting debris formed Saturn's rings.

choices.text: According to researchers' simulations, debris from Saturn's rings would likely have caused Chrysalis—a large moon orbiting Saturn—to break apart around 160 million years ago.
In a 2022 study, researchers proposed that Saturn was once orbited by a large moon that broke apart.
Around 160 million years ago, a large moon (Chrysalis) may have come close enough to Saturn that tidal forces broke the moon apart; its debris then formed the planet's rings.
If a moon orbiting a planet (like Saturn) comes close enough to that planet, tidal forces can cause the moon to break apart.

• It is identifiable by its dark violet ear patches and its short, black, straight bill.
• The little hermit is a small hummingbird found in Suriname.
• It is identifiable by its white central tail feathers and its long, black, curved bill.
passage:
Which choice most effectively uses information from the given sentences to emphasize a similarity between the two birds?
While researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes:
• *Birds of Northern South America* is an identification guidebook by ornithologists Daniel Harper, Maria Moreno, and Rafael Castillo.
• It lists the thirty-five hummingbird species found in Suriname.
• The brown violetear is a large hummingbird found in Suriname.
• It is identifiable by its dark violet ear patches and its short, black, straight bill.

choices.text: While each exhibits unique traits of its own, the brown violetear and the little hermit both have black bills.
The little hermit is a small hummingbird identifiable by its white central tail feathers and its long, black, curved bill.
Though they share several traits in common, the brown violetear is larger than the little hermit.
*Birds of Northern South America* is an expertly compiled guidebook by ornithologists Daniel Harper, Maria Moreno, and Rafael Castillo.

subject: reading
prompt:
The student wants to emphasize the effect media had on building Wong Fei-hung's legacy. Which choice most effectively uses relevant information from the notes to accomplish this goal?
While researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes:
• Little is known about the life of Wong Fei-hung (1847-1925).
• He was born near Foshan, China, and gained local recognition as a physician and Hung Ga (also known as Hung Gar) Kung Fu master.
• He achieved many remarkable martial arts feats—some confirmed and some rumored.
• He has become an internationally known folk hero thanks to his depiction in over a hundred films, television shows, and other media.
• In the 1991 film *Once Upon a Time in China*, actor Jet Li portrays Wong Feihung using superhuman kung fu abilities to save his community.
passage:
The student wants to emphasize the effect media had on building Wong Fei-hung's legacy. Which choice most effectively uses relevant information from the notes to accomplish this goal?
While researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes:
• Little is known about the life of Wong Fei-hung (1847-1925).
• He was born near Foshan, China, and gained local recognition as a physician and Hung Ga (also known as Hung Gar) Kung Fu master.
• He achieved many remarkable martial arts feats—some confirmed and some rumored.
• He has become an internationally known folk hero thanks to his depiction in over a hundred films, television shows, and other media.
• In the 1991 film *Once Upon a Time in China*, actor Jet Li portrays Wong Feihung using superhuman kung fu abilities to save his community.

choices.text: Thanks to his depiction in over a hundred pieces of media, Wong Fei-hung was locally known as a successful physician and Hung Ga Kung Fu master.
Wong Fei-hung's abilities as a kung fu master are depicted in many media, including the 1991 film *Once Upon a Time in China*.
Various media have depicted Wong Fei-hung, the successful physician and kung fu master who became an internationally known folk hero.
Though he was known locally during his lifetime, Wong Fei-hung's later depiction in television, film, and other media has turned him into an internationally known folk hero.

subject: reading
prompt:
Which finding from the model tests, if true, would support the researchers' hypothesis?
Throughout history, tall structures such as Qutb Minar in India or Ryounkaku in Japan have posed a challenge to their architects, since these structures are more susceptible to physical forces than smaller structures. For example, the lower floors of a building must be able to support the enormous weight of the upper floors. To test a hypothesis that steel is more suitable than concrete for construction of a tall building's lower floors, researchers created a computer model of two buildings that were identical except that one had concrete lower floors while one had steel lower floors, and simulated the effects of the forces of wind and gravity on those structures over the course of a century.
passage: Throughout history, tall structures such as Qutb Minar in India or Ryounkaku in Japan have posed a challenge to their architects, since these structures are more susceptible to physical forces than smaller structures. For example, the lower floors of a building must be able to support the enormous weight of the upper floors. To test a hypothesis that steel is more suitable than concrete for construction of a tall building's lower floors, researchers created a computer model of two buildings that were identical except that one had concrete lower floors while one had steel lower floors, and simulated the effects of the forces of wind and gravity on those structures over the course of a century.
questionText: Which finding from the model tests, if true, would support the researchers' hypothesis?

choices.text: During the simulation, the structure with steel lower floors bent in high wind to a greater degree than did the structure with concrete lower floors.
At the end of the simulation, the structure with concrete lower floors was several inches shorter than the structure with steel lower floors.

During the simulation, when sudden forces caused vibrations in the structures, those vibrations took longer to disappear in the structure with steel lower floors than in the structure with concrete lower floors.
At the end of the simulation, the structure with steel lower floors had slightly larger dimensions at its base than it did at the beginning of the simulation.

subject: reading
prompt:
Which choice best states the function of the underlined sentence in the text as a whole?
Vertical gene transfer involves the transmission of genetic material from a parent to offspring; horizontal gene transfer, on the other hand, involves the exchange of genetic material between organisms not in a parent-offspring relationship. <u>While horizontal gene transfer is common among prokaryotes—single-celled organisms, such as the bacteria *Brevibacillus borstelensis* and *Lactococcus lactis*—it has rarely been observed among eukaryotes (organisms whose cells contain nuclei).</u> However, new studies suggest that horizontal gene transfer is more common in eukaryotes than originally thought.
passage: Vertical gene transfer involves the transmission of genetic material from a parent to offspring; horizontal gene transfer, on the other hand, involves the exchange of genetic material between organisms not in a parent-offspring relationship. <u>While horizontal gene transfer is common among prokaryotes—single-celled organisms, such as the bacteria *Brevibacillus borstelensis* and *Lactococcus lactis*—it has rarely been observed among eukaryotes (organisms whose cells contain nuclei).</u> However, new studies suggest that horizontal gene transfer is more common in eukaryotes than originally thought.
questionText: Which choice best states the function of the underlined sentence in the text as a whole?

choices.text: It indicates a distinction between the mechanics of two kinds of biological processes.
It contrasts the frequency with which a biological phenomenon has been detected in two categories of organisms.
It explains why a common perception of a biological process is flawed.
It argues that two biological phenomena are more similar than they may initially appear to be.

subject: reading
prompt:
Based on the texts, the author of Text 1 and the author of Text 2 would most likely agree with which statement?
Text 1
In separate studies, Laura Benton and Michael R. Sato and Priya Nair and colleagues examined whether plants transfer nutrients to one another using a common mycorrhizal network (CMN)—a lattice of fungal strands in the soil. Benton and Sato excluded all pathways other than the CMN by using barriers to keep the plants' root systems separate while allowing mycorrhizal strands through—a crucial step Nair and colleagues' study did not take.
Text 2
Benton and Sato took the necessary precaution of separating the plants' root systems (thereby excluding root-to-root transmission). However, any barrier used must allow the thread-like hyphae of a CMN to pass through, and this permeability would also allow liquids through. Thus, the researchers' experimental design cannot ensure that any nutrient transfer observed can be attributed to a CMN and not to some other pathway.
passage:
Text 1
In separate studies, Laura Benton and Michael R. Sato and Priya Nair and colleagues examined whether plants transfer nutrients to one another using a common mycorrhizal network (CMN)—a lattice of fungal strands in the soil. Benton and Sato excluded all pathways other than the CMN by using barriers to keep the plants' root systems separate while allowing mycorrhizal strands through—a crucial step Nair and colleagues' study did not take.
Text 2
Benton and Sato took the necessary precaution of separating the plants' root systems (thereby excluding root-to-root transmission). However, any barrier used must allow the thread-like hyphae of a CMN to pass through, and this permeability would also allow liquids through. Thus, the researchers' experimental design cannot ensure that any nutrient transfer observed can be attributed to a CMN and not to some other pathway.
questionText: Based on the texts, the author of Text 1 and the author of Text 2 would most likely agree with which statement?

choices.text: The barriers used in Benton and Sato's study effectively excluded root-to-root transmission of nutrients.
A barrier that is impervious to both roots and fungal strands is necessary to evaluate nutrient transfer via a CMN.
Excluding root-to-root transfer of nutrients between plants is sufficient to ensure that any observed nutrient transfer must involve a CMN.
Nair and colleagues' study did not find convincing evidence of nutrient transfer between individual plants.

subject: reading
prompt:
Which choice best describes the finding made by Lin and colleagues, as presented in the text?
Optimal foraging theory (OFT) holds that animals' foraging behaviors reflect cost-benefit trade-offs that vary by species and with dynamic ecological circumstances. One such circumstance is lunar intensity, which Joel Rivera and colleagues found to be negatively associated with foraging by Arizona pocket mice but Maya K. Lin and colleagues found to be positively associated with foraging by mongoose lemurs. This discrepancy is explicable in terms of OFT: the lemurs' greater reliance on vision means that higher lunar intensity benefits them more than it benefits the mice.
passage: Optimal foraging theory (OFT) holds that animals' foraging behaviors reflect cost-benefit trade-offs that vary by species and with dynamic ecological circumstances. One such circumstance is lunar intensity, which Joel Rivera and colleagues found to be negatively associated with foraging by Arizona pocket mice but Maya K. Lin and colleagues found to be positively associated with foraging by mongoose lemurs. This discrepancy is explicable in terms of OFT: the lemurs' greater reliance on vision means that higher lunar intensity benefits them more than it benefits the mice.
questionText: Which choice best describes the finding made by Lin and colleagues, as presented in the text?

choices.text: Increased lunar intensity is more beneficial for mongoose lemurs than it is for Arizona pocket mice.
As lunar intensity increases, mongoose lemurs increase their foraging activity.
During periods of high lunar intensity, mongoose lemurs increase their reliance on vision.
As lunar intensity increases, Arizona pocket mice reduce their foraging activity.

Considering over three years' worth of data, the researchers found that white butterflies were observed on green flowers more often than any other butterflies were, and that butterflies were observed to feed more often on cloudy days than on other days.

subject: reading
prompt:
Which finding, if true, would most directly support Rivera and colleagues' conclusion?
The bird species *Myiobius barbatus* (the bearded flycatcher) shares some territory in French Guiana with *Thamnomanes caesius* (the cinereous antshrike), which emits a loud alarm call when it detects predators. Biologist Nadia Rivera and colleagues recorded *T. caesius* alarm calls and played them in the vicinity of wild *M. barbatus*. Finding that the birds often froze in place or scattered into vegetation upon hearing the calls, they concluded that *M. barbatus* associates *T. caesius* alarm calls with danger.
passage: The bird species *Myiobius barbatus* (the bearded flycatcher) shares some territory in French Guiana with *Thamnomanes caesius* (the cinereous antshrike), which emits a loud alarm call when it detects predators. Biologist Nadia Rivera and colleagues recorded *T. caesius* alarm calls and played them in the vicinity of wild *M. barbatus*. Finding that the birds often froze in place or scattered into vegetation upon hearing the calls, they concluded that *M. barbatus* associates *T. caesius* alarm calls with danger.
questionText: Which finding, if true, would most directly support Rivera and colleagues' conclusion?

choices.text: Bird species other than *M. barbatus* also showed a tendency to freeze in place or scatter into vegetation when Rivera and colleagues played *T. caesius* alarm calls.
Rivera and colleagues played alarm calls from different *T. caesius* individuals and observed no significant variation in the responses of *M. barbatus*.
In some instances, *M. barbatus* froze in place or scattered into vegetation when Rivera and colleagues approached but before they began playing sounds.
When Rivera and colleagues played control sounds of random noise in the vicinity of *M. barbatus*, the birds displayed no reaction.

subject: reading
prompt:
Which finding, if true, would most directly support Petrenko and colleagues' claim?
The linguistic niche hypothesis (LNH) posits that the exotericity of languages (how prevalent non-native speakers are) and grammatical complexity are inversely related, which the LNH ascribes to attrition of complex grammatical rules as more non-native speakers adopt the language but fail to acquire those rules. Focusing on two characteristics that are positive indices of grammatical complexity, fusion (when new phonemes arise from the merger of previously distinct ones) and informativity (languages' capacity for meaningful variation), Daria Petrenko and colleagues conducted a quantitative analysis for more than 1,300 languages and claim the outcome is inconsistent with the LNH.
passage: The linguistic niche hypothesis (LNH) posits that the exotericity of languages (how prevalent non-native speakers are) and grammatical complexity are inversely related, which the LNH ascribes to attrition of complex grammatical rules as more non-native speakers adopt the language but fail to acquire those rules. Focusing on two characteristics that are positive indices of grammatical complexity, fusion (when new phonemes arise from the merger of previously distinct ones) and informativity (languages' capacity for meaningful variation), Daria Petrenko and colleagues conducted a quantitative analysis for more than 1,300 languages and claim the outcome is inconsistent with the LNH.
questionText: Which finding, if true, would most directly support Petrenko and colleagues' claim?

choices.text: Petrenko and colleagues' analysis showed a slightly positive correlation between grammatical complexity and fusion.
Petrenko and colleagues' analysis showed a slightly negative correlation between grammatical complexity and exotericity.
Petrenko and colleagues' analysis showed a slightly positive correlation between fusion and exotericity and between informativity and exotericity.
Petrenko and colleagues' analysis showed a slightly negative correlation between grammatical complexity and fusion and between grammatical complexity and informativity.

questionText: Which quotation from *The Age of Innocence* best illustrates the claim?

choices.text: "[May] dropped her eyes to the immense bouquet of lilies-of-the-valley on her knee, and Newland Archer saw her white-gloved finger-tips touch the flowers softly."
"Newland Archer, leaning against the wall at the back of the club box [where his seat was], turned his eyes from the stage and scanned the opposite side of the house."

"Though there was already talk...of a new Opera House which should compete in costliness and splendour with those of the great European capitals, the world of fashion was still content to reassemble every winter in the shabby red and gold boxes of the sociable old Academy [of Music]."
"Already [Newland's] imagination, leaping ahead of the engagement ring, the betrothal kiss and the [wedding] march from Lohengrin, pictured [May] at his side."

subject: reading
prompt:
Which choice most logically completes the text?
A group of primate conservationists recently began a long-term study of the effects of different conservation strategies on the white-headed langur (*Trachypithecus poliocephalus*). The species population is currently estimated to be around 1,000. It is challenging to accurately count these primates, however, which makes it difficult to tell whether the population is increasing, decreasing, or staying stable. The study may thus ______
passage: A group of primate conservationists recently began a long-term study of the effects of different conservation strategies on the white-headed langur (*Trachypithecus poliocephalus*). The species population is currently estimated to be around 1,000. It is challenging to accurately count these primates, however, which makes it difficult to tell whether the population is increasing, decreasing, or staying stable. The study may thus ______
questionText: Which choice most logically completes the text?

choices.text: risk making inaccurate conclusions about the effectiveness of different conservation strategies.
cause other conservationists to adopt a new methodology for counting populations.
benefit from including species beyond the white-headed langur.
fail to consider less-well-known conservation approaches for the white-headed langur.

subject: reading
prompt:
Which choice most logically completes the text?
The musical *Hadestown* was produced Off-Broadway in New York in 2016. A revised version of the musical premiered on Broadway in 2019, in a larger production. In a review of the Broadway production, theater critic Daniel Moreno enthusiastically praised the musical's storytelling. However, Moreno also explained that he had seen the earlier version of *Hadestown* in 2016 and had found the storytelling to be very confusing. This suggests that in Moreno's view, ______
passage: The musical *Hadestown* was produced Off-Broadway in New York in 2016. A revised version of the musical premiered on Broadway in 2019, in a larger production. In a review of the Broadway production, theater critic Daniel Moreno enthusiastically praised the musical's storytelling. However, Moreno also explained that he had seen the earlier version of *Hadestown* in 2016 and had found the storytelling to be very confusing. This suggests that in Moreno's view, ______
questionText: Which choice most logically completes the text?

choices.text: *Hadestown* improved greatly between 2016 and its premiere on Broadway.
*Hadestown* should have had a larger production in 2019 than it actually did.
the 2019 version of *Hadestown* was less enjoyable than the 2016 version.
the 2016 version of *Hadestown* had fewer storytelling problems than the 2019 version did.

subject: reading
prompt:
Which choice most logically completes the text?
Biochemists R. Nisha Menon and Leo J. Hartman studied the mass and heat transfer processes that occur when foods, such as the Indian snacks dahibara aludam and paneer pakora, are fried in oil. During frying, water in the crust evaporates, leaving voids that oil can fill, thereby increasing the food's fat content. As the process continues, water from the food's center moves to the crust as long as the crust remains permeable. Therefore, the less moisture a food loses during frying, ______
passage: Biochemists R. Nisha Menon and Leo J. Hartman studied the mass and heat transfer processes that occur when foods, such as the Indian snacks dahibara aludam and paneer pakora, are fried in oil. During frying, water in the crust evaporates, leaving voids that oil can fill, thereby increasing the food's fat content. As the process continues, water from the food's center moves to the crust as long as the crust remains permeable. Therefore, the less moisture a food loses during frying, ______
questionText: Which choice most logically completes the text?

choices.text: the crispier the crust will be when frying is completed.
the less the fat content is increased through frying.
the slower the crust will lose its permeability.
the higher the temperature must be to fry the food.

subject: reading
prompt:
The student wants to provide an example of a sky island. Which choice most effectively uses relevant information from the notes to accomplish this goal?
While researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes:
• The Los Ajos-La Madera Mountains are a mountain range located in northwestern Mexico.
• The range is one of the dozens of "sky islands" in the southwestern US and northwestern Mexico.
• A sky island is an isolated mountain range whose environment differs drastically from that of the surrounding lowlands.
• The National Park Service (NPS) said, "The mountains are 'islands' surrounded by deserts that are 'seas'"
• The NPS said, "Each sky island is a unique ecosystem."
passage:
The student wants to provide an example of a sky island. Which choice most effectively uses relevant information from the notes to accomplish this goal?
While researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes:
• The Los Ajos-La Madera Mountains are a mountain range located in northwestern Mexico.
• The range is one of the dozens of "sky islands" in the southwestern US and northwestern Mexico.
• A sky island is an isolated mountain range whose environment differs drastically from that of the surrounding lowlands.
• The National Park Service (NPS) said, "The mountains are 'islands' surrounded by deserts that are 'seas'"
• The NPS said, "Each sky island is a unique ecosystem."

choices.text: In the southwestern US and northwestern Mexico, there are dozens of sky islands: isolated mountain ranges whose environments differ drastically from those of the surrounding lowlands.
The NPS considers each of the sky islands, whose environments differ drastically from those of the surrounding lowlands, to be a "unique ecosystem."
There are dozens of sky islands in the southwestern US and northwestern Mexico, such as the Los Ajos-La Madera Mountains.

Sky islands are isolated mountain ranges whose environments differ drastically from those of the surrounding lowlands; as the NPS puts it, "the mountains are 'islands' surrounded by deserts that are 'seas'."

subject: reading
prompt:
The student wants to contrast the two samples. Which choice most effectively uses relevant information from the notes to accomplish this goal?
While researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes:
• Generally, an object will heat up when twisted.
• The twisting of an object is known as torsion.
• A 2019 study led by Mina Park and Daniel Foster tested the torsional heating of various fibers.
• When a 3-millimeter-thick sample of thermoplastic polyurethane (TPU) fiber was twisted, its average surface temperature increased by 6°C.
• When a 4-millimeter-thick sample of styrene-ethylene-butylene-styrene (SEBS) rubber fiber was twisted, its average surface temperature increased by 3.5°C.
passage:
The student wants to contrast the two samples. Which choice most effectively uses relevant information from the notes to accomplish this goal?
While researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes:
• Generally, an object will heat up when twisted.
• The twisting of an object is known as torsion.
• A 2019 study led by Mina Park and Daniel Foster tested the torsional heating of various fibers.
• When a 3-millimeter-thick sample of thermoplastic polyurethane (TPU) fiber was twisted, its average surface temperature increased by 6°C.
• When a 4-millimeter-thick sample of styrene-ethylene-butylene-styrene (SEBS) rubber fiber was twisted, its average surface temperature increased by 3.5°C.

choices.text: Twisting an object will generally cause its temperature to increase, a process known as torsional heating.
In 2019, researchers studied the effect of torsional heating on various fibers, including samples of SEBS rubber and TPU.
When the fibers were twisted as part of the 2019 study, the surface temperature of both samples increased.
The SEBS rubber sample used in the 2019 study was thicker than the TPU sample.
